label: The Fake
description: Age 17. Forged a birth certificate. Call centre at 24/7, UK shift 4PM–1AM. ₹8,000 a month. He traded penny stocks with what was left.

label: The First Trade
description: Marsoft. A penny stock tech company. Bought at ₹4, sold at ₹20. 'There was no reason why it should have gone up. It was blind luck. But I was hooked.'

label: Father's Leap of Faith
description: His father, a Canara Bank executive, handed over his personal savings. No questions asked. The same man who watched his son drop out of school.

label: The Manager's Deal
description: He got so good at trading that his call centre manager stopped requiring him to show up. Just marked him present. Let him manage the entire team's money.

label: Kamath & Associates
description: With Nithin. A proprietary trading and sub-broking firm. HNI portfolios. Over the next decade: 55% post-tax returns, year on year.

label: 2008: The System Breaks
description: The global financial crisis revealed what they already suspected: India's brokerage system was opaque, expensive, and designed to extract — not serve.

label: Zerodha — August 15, 2010
description: Independence Day. Not a coincidence. Zero + Rodha (Sanskrit: barrier) = Zero Barriers. ₹10 lakh from personal savings. No VC. No ads. No permission.

label: Word of Mouth Only
description: 3,000 accounts in year one. Growing entirely through referrals. Never bought a single ad. Kailash Nadh building the entire tech stack from scratch.

label: Kite: Trading Reimagined
description: Their HTML5 trading platform. Fast. Clean. No dark patterns. The product that made everyone else look like they were designed in 2001.

label: Forbes 30 Under 30
description: Asia, Finance & Venture Capital. The dropout who disrupted an entire industry got noticed. He still hadn't spent a rupee on advertising.

label: True Beacon — Zero Fee
description: Co-founded with Richard Pattle (former Standard Chartered). Minimum ₹10 crore to invest. Zero management fees. Only 10% performance fee on profits. GIFT City's first hedge fund.

label: The Pandemic Paradox
description: While the world locked down, retail India discovered trading. Zerodha doubled clients. The man who built for exactly this moment watched it arrive.

label: Chess-Gate
description: Charity chess against Viswanathan Anand. Accuracy: 98.9%. His normal Chess.com games: 0.6–10.9%. Chess.com banned him. Forbes called it 'Liar's Chess'. Anand forgave him in 24 hours.

label: India's Youngest Billionaire
description: Age 34. School dropout. No degree. Never raised external capital. The ₹8,000 call centre salary had become $1.5 billion.

label: 50% Away
description: Age 36. India's youngest Giving Pledge signatory. 'I cannot think of a more personally rewarding use of wealth than to give while one is living.' He also built YIPP — a junior version for Indians under 45.

label: Building the Next Generation
description: WTFund: ₹20 lakh grants, no equity, for founders under 25. The Foundery: 90-day residential co-founder factory with Kishore Biyani. 'MBAs create managers. We need builders.'

label: Modi. Musk. ₹27,000 Crore.
description: January 2025: PM Modi's first-ever podcast. November 2025: Elon Musk, 7 million views. The school dropout from Shimoga is now India's most significant media interviewer.
